rationales.self_direction: He emphasizes building the business "on my own terms" with "nobody telling me which clients to take or how many hours to bill," showing a strong preference for autonomy.
rationales.achievement: Closing the deal exactly as he wanted and reflecting that it was "worth every sleepless night" shows active pursuit of competence and success.
rationales.power: He notes that the other side "came around because they knew I'd walk and they needed me more than I needed them," highlighting leverage and control in the negotiation.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects Derek's advice and says he does not want "someone else's version of success stapled to my forehead," clearly defending his own path.
rationales.conformity: The entry centers on resisting family expectations about career and life milestones, and he shows no interest in adjusting himself to fit their preferred script.

rationales.self_direction: He walks away from a steady client because the committees and bureaucracy violate why he "started this company," prioritizing independence over accommodation.
rationales.security: He explicitly gives up money that "was steady" and refuses the safer predictable arrangement, even though resetting would be the lower-risk choice.

rationales.self_direction: He says yes because they gave him "full creative control over the architecture" and avoided "corporate layers," showing clear preference for autonomous work.
rationales.achievement: Being treated "like an expert, not a vendor" and trusted to build the solution reflects competence being recognized and exercised.

rationales.self_direction: He spends the day "figuring things out myself even when it's slower," showing a strong need to solve problems independently.
rationales.achievement: He says outside help feels "borrowed" and that he did not really "earn the solution," tying worth to personally mastering the task.

rationales.self_direction: He celebrates that "I control the timeline" and would still choose this life over "a cubicle with more," showing clear commitment to self-directed work.
rationales.hedonism: The long walk, Thai food, and smiling at the fact that this is "my life" show active enjoyment and savoring of the present.
rationales.achievement: He reports "solid progress," finishing the schema, and earning sign-off on the prototype, which are concrete markers of competent progress.

rationales.hedonism: He is willing to keep "working through the holidays" after sleeping four hours a night, showing active self-denial rather than present enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: He refuses to reset expectations because "I quoted the timeline. I own it," prioritizing meeting a demanding standard despite the cost.
rationales.benevolence: He admits he snapped at the barista and lied about Christmas dinner, while hearing Rachel's disappointment, showing his choices are straining close relationships.

rationales.hedonism: After finishing the project, he chooses to take "the rest of the week off," which is a direct move toward rest and relief.
rationales.benevolence: He calls Rachel to apologize for Christmas and says he might actually answer when she calls, showing renewed effort toward a close relationship.

rationales.achievement: He stays late to keep "running another simulation batch," framing the sacrifice as part of finishing the PhD for the future.
rationales.benevolence: He misses Layla's first swimming lesson and worries about the day "they stop asking me to come," showing his work choices are failing close family commitments.

rationales.achievement: He accepts a tight revision deadline and spends the day on necessary lab work, showing continued focus on academic performance.
rationales.benevolence: He admits he should have gone to the pediatrician visit and that leaning on Dina for everything is no longer real partnership, indicating neglect of close others.
He reads to Layla, holds Omar as he falls asleep, and commits to handling the kids so Dina can take her class, prioritizing his family's needs and support.

rationales.hedonism: He pulls "two all-nighters" and says his "body is wrecked," showing active disregard for comfort and rest.
rationales.achievement: Submitting the revision at 4am after an intense push reflects strong commitment to completing a demanding academic goal.
rationales.benevolence: He snaps at Dina and then recognizes she "doesn't deserve my stress dumped on her," showing his behavior is undermining a close relationship.

rationales.hedonism: He enjoys a relaxed family morning with pancakes, cartoons, and being present instead of working, choosing immediate warmth over more productivity.
rationales.benevolence: He gives Dina a real apology and spends the morning with the kids, clearly investing care and repair into his closest relationships.

rationales.achievement: He feels good that the advisor approved the chapter and immediately starts outlining the next one, reflecting steady progress toward academic success.
rationales.security: He says "this arrangement is working and I want to protect it," showing an explicit desire to preserve a stable family routine.
rationales.benevolence: Taking the kids to the park while Dina is at class and appreciating that the setup works show active support for his family.

rationales.achievement: He measures himself against "conference papers, publications" and "CV metrics that actually matter for the job market," showing clear focus on external performance standards.

rationales.benevolence: He feels he "failed them" because his parents could not lean on him, and he immediately tells his advisor he may need to go to Amman, showing concern and willingness to respond to family need.
